description:
src/plugin/limits.rs:123 shells out to /bin/kill -KILL -{pid} to kill the plugin process group on timeout. This passes locally but hangs indefinitely in GitHub Actions (tests plugin_sync_timeout_is_killed_and_warned and plugin_push_timeout_is_killed_and_warned). Root cause: the 'kill -KILL -123' argument form is ambiguous across kill implementations, the error is swallowed by 'let _', and if the kill fails the subsequent child.wait() + reader-thread join block indefinitely because the shell-forked 'sleep 20' grandchild still holds the stdout/stderr pipe write ends.

Fix: replace the shell-out with libc::killpg(pid, SIGKILL). libc is already in Cargo.lock as an indirect dep so adding it as a direct dep is zero cost. As defense-in-depth, after kill explicitly drop any remaining references that keep the stdout/stderr fds open so the reader threads are guaranteed to see EOF.

Verification: locally the 3 tests pass in ~2s. The fix must NOT regress local behavior. CI run must complete within the timeout-minutes: 6 cap.
title: Plugin runner: kill_process_group should use libc::killpg
